text:
Harper loved mysteries. She would read anything. One sunny afternoon, wearing her favorite blue scarf because it smelled like jasmine blooms, Harper picked up a battered detective novel her grandmother loved.

It talked about spooky old cottages. Someone dead. Something mysterious. Harper thought owl eyes glow purple.

She walked. Owls? Nope. That gooseberry bush had berries?

She decided to sneak some. A hoot. Nothing. She pecked. Nothing. Gooseberries weren't poisoned.

That night she dreampt. She wore feathers. Soft feathers. They whispered secrets.

She laughed. Sleep.

The moon. Owls.

She woke. Sunburnt. Goose.

She found feathers downtown. A bluebird? That'd do. She tied them. Owls love moonlight.

No spooky stuff. Just dreams.

Her favorite thing. Owls.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
